story-context.title: Testcontainers Keycloak for Integration Tests
story-context.resequenced: 2025-11-09 (moved from 3.10 to 3.3 for Constitutional TDD compliance)
story-context.summary: Provision Keycloak Testcontainer 26.4.2 with complete realm configuration (eaf realm, eaf-api client, test users with roles, tenant_id attribute mapper) and generateToken() utility for security integration tests. This story enables all Epic 3 integration tests to use real Keycloak authentication instead of mocking JWT validation.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Testcontainers Keycloak 26.4.2 configured in test dependencies
KeycloakTestContainer.kt utility creates container with realm import and generateToken() method
realm-export.json configured (eaf realm, eaf-api client, test users, roles, tenant_id mapper)
Test realm includes users: admin@eaf.com (WIDGET_ADMIN, ADMIN), viewer@eaf.com (WIDGET_VIEWER)
Test realm includes roles: WIDGET_ADMIN, WIDGET_VIEWER, ADMIN
application-test.yml configured with Keycloak Admin Client properties (admin-cli, admin password)
Container reuse enabled for performance (start once per test class)
Container-generated JWTs for authentication tests (via KeycloakBuilder with password grant)
All security integration tests pass using Testcontainers Keycloak

sections.section: Lines 749-786: KeycloakTestContainer.kt with complete generateToken() implementation
Lines 888-917: application-test.yml with Keycloak Admin Client config

example.name: KeycloakTestContainer.kt
example.description: COMPLETE: Keycloak Testcontainer with realm import and JWT generation

example.name: realm-export.json
example.description: Complete Keycloak test realm configuration with users, roles, tenant_id mapper

example.name: SecuredWidgetApiTest.kt
example.description: Example integration test using Testcontainers Keycloak

implementation-sequence.step: Add testcontainers-keycloak and keycloak-admin-client test dependencies
Configure test users (admin@eaf.com, viewer@eaf.com) with passwords and tenant_id attributes
Configure tenant_id client scope with attribute mapper
Create KeycloakTestContainer.kt object with singleton pattern
Implement generateToken() using KeycloakBuilder with password grant
Configure application-test.yml with Keycloak Admin Client properties
Add @DynamicPropertySource to set issuer-uri and jwk-set-uri dynamically
Write integration tests for both admin and viewer roles
Validate container reuse works across test classes

container-setup.pattern: Singleton object pattern (start once, reuse everywhere)

jwt-generation.expected-claims: sub, iss, aud, exp, iat, tenant_id, realm_access.roles

common-pitfalls.pitfall: realm-export.json not found (incorrect path in withRealmImportFile)
Missing tenant_id attribute in test users (Layer 4 validation fails)
Container not started in beforeSpec (tests fail with connection refused)

definition-of-done.item: realm-export.json complete with 2 test users, 3 roles, tenant_id mapper
Integration tests pass using real Keycloak JWTs
application-test.yml includes Keycloak Admin Client properties
